There seems to be no information available on the plant 'Nastus tjankorreh'. However, I can provide information about the Nasturtium plant which shares similarities with the name you provided.

The Nasturtium plant (Tropaeolum majus), also known as Indian cress, is a flowering plant that belongs to the cress family (Brassicaceae). The leaves and stems of the Nasturtium plant have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system.

The bioactive constituents of the Nasturtium plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

The most common herbal preparations made from the Nasturtium plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
